bindings.quest_action: fetch materials that require both crafters to process
bindings.quest_focus: the blacksmith-cook rivalry paralyzing the settlement
bindings.quest_stakes: half the settlement going hungry and the other half wielding dull blades
bindings.quest_threat: two stubborn artisans refusing to cooperate
bindings.quest_origin: a petty contest over who contributes more to the settlement

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}! Perfect, an impartial judge. You have no idea how badly we need one.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The blacksmith and the cook have been locked in a contest for weeks: each claims they contribute more to {location} than the other. Normally it's harmless bickering, but now they've escalated. The blacksmith refuses to repair the cook's tools, and the cook won't feed anyone who takes the blacksmith's side. {target_npc} says if someone doesn't settle this, {quest_stakes}. They need {quest_item} that neither can source alone: proof that they need each other.
dialogueChunks.outro: Bring back the {quest_item} and maybe we can knock some sense into both of them.

playerResponses.text: Stubborn craftspeople? I'll find what they need and teach them a lesson in cooperation.
I'll fetch your supplies, but keeping the peace is your job.
I'm not a mediator. Let them sort it out themselves.

bindings.quest_action: find the missing apprentice and recover the materials
bindings.quest_focus: a harvest festival wager between two artisans
bindings.quest_stakes: a missing apprentice lost in dangerous territory
bindings.quest_threat: a rivalry that has stopped both artisans from doing their jobs
bindings.quest_origin: a wager over the title of Master Artisan

dialogueChunks.intro: You wouldn't believe the mess this town is in, {player_name}. Two artisans trying to outdo each other and everyone else paying the price.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Both our blacksmith and our cook entered a wager: whoever produces the finer work by the harvest festival wins the title of Master Artisan of {location}. The problem is, they're both so focused on winning that they've stopped doing their actual jobs. Nobody can get a blade sharpened or a decent meal. Worse, the blacksmith sent someone {location_hint} to gather {quest_item} for their masterpiece and they haven't come back. The {enemy_type} in that area have been aggressive lately. Someone needs to {quest_action}.
dialogueChunks.outro: Find the apprentice and the materials. Maybe once both artisans have what they need, they'll stop holding the settlement hostage.

playerResponses.text: A missing apprentice takes priority. I'll head out now.
Rescuing someone caught in a petty rivalry? I suppose I've done stranger things.
Their competition, their problem. I've got my own business.

bindings.quest_action: find a new supply source large enough for both traders
bindings.quest_focus: exclusive trading rights in the tavern
bindings.quest_threat: two rival traders escalating a commercial dispute into violence

dialogueChunks.intro: Listen, {player_name}, I run a tavern, not a boxing ring. But that's what my common room has turned into.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Two rival traders have been competing for {quest_focus} in {location}. Their arguments turned into shoving matches, and last night one of them smashed a table over the other's goods. {target_npc} is furious. Now both traders are demanding I ban the other from the tavern, and their supporters are taking sides. The only way to cool things down is to prove there's enough supply for both: someone needs to {quest_action} {location_hint}.
dialogueChunks.outro: Find enough {quest_item} to show these fools there's room for everyone, before {quest_stakes}.

playerResponses.text: I'd rather brave the wilds than sit through another tavern brawl. I'm on it.
Merchant disputes and broken furniture. What's in it for me?
Not my tavern, not my problem.

bindings.quest_action: gather materials so both crafters can build competing prototypes
bindings.quest_stakes: the settlement left without a desperately needed watchtower
bindings.quest_threat: two crafters holding essential construction hostage over ego
bindings.quest_origin: competing architectural designs submitted to the council

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I could use someone with no stake in the outcome. Everyone here is picking sides and it's gotten out of hand.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Our two best crafters both submitted designs for {quest_focus} at {location}. The council can't agree on which plan to use, and now neither crafter will work on anything else until their design is chosen. {target_npc} suggested a test: whoever can produce a working prototype using {quest_item} sourced from the old ruins {location_hint} proves their design is practical. But the ruins are deep in {enemy_type} territory, and neither crafter will set foot outside the walls.
dialogueChunks.outro: If you {quest_action}, we can hold a fair test and end this deadlock. The settlement needs that watchtower.

playerResponses.text: A fair test sounds reasonable. I'll get the materials for both.
Fetching supplies so two stubborn crafters can compete? At least the watchtower matters.
Build it yourselves. I don't settle craft disputes.
